completion_criteria_checked: docs/BRR_POLICY.md gains a 'Verification Depth Policy' section defining light/normal/strict rigor levels with concrete, distinguishing criteria for each. -- MET: read the full added section text directly from the file (not a diff summary); each level states concretely what evidence/checks it requires.

The section ties each level to Task Safety Class (A/B/C/D) and to task type via an explicit mapping, not left to case-by-case judgment. -- MET: an 8-row Class x task-type table maps every combination to a depth or an explicit 'not applicable' with reasoning.

The mapping is reconciled against existing canonical policy (Task Safety Classification, Acceptance Boundary Rules, Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy) with no contradiction introduced. -- MET: re-read Task Safety Classification and Acceptance Boundary Rules directly; confirmed the new section's claim that a truth-surface task cannot be Class A is consistent with the Owner Review Matrix row 7 / Class C mapping already in the doc, and confirmed the 'Relationship to Acceptance Boundary Rules' subsection correctly states the two policies answer different questions (self-acceptance eligibility vs. review rigor) without overlap or contradiction.
A new decision is recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md documenting the policy addition. -- MET: DECISION-046 present, correctly cross-referenced.

No runtime script, schema, enforcement, or gating change is introduced. -- MET: git diff confined to docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/DECISIONS.md, and the two live state files; no file under scripts/ or schemas/ was touched.

No existing verdict, task safety class, or stop condition is redefined or weakened. -- MET: the new section explicitly states it introduces no new verdict and reuses existing classes; spot-checked against Task Safety Classification and Stop-Instead-of-Guess Policy text, no redefinition found.

risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback (Codex unavailable). No independent second-party (Codex) review occurred. GPT secondary review: not performed this cycle.

This task is Class B and truth-surface-affecting, which by its own new policy requires 'strict' depth; strict depth was applied, but strict depth performed by the same party who authored the change is a narrower independent check than a genuinely separate reviewer would provide -- the same self-refereeing limitation already disclosed for other BRR policy cycles under this fallback.

The policy is definitional only and not yet fielded into any script; nothing currently enforces that future verification cycles actually apply the depth this table calls for. That gap is inherent to a policy-only deliverable and is named explicitly in the section's own 'Notes on scope', not hidden.

The DECISION-036 commit-and-push-every-PASS authorization was time-boxed to 'the remainder of BRR Phase 2' and has lapsed now that PCC is in BRR Phase 3 (per DECISION-036's own text). This cycle's changes are being committed locally per the standing DECISION-020 default; pushing to any remote requires a fresh, explicit owner approval this cycle, not an assumption that DECISION-036 still covers it.
summary: Verified pcc-brr3-001 (BRR Policy: Verification Depth) at 'strict' depth, as the task's own new policy requires for a Class B, truth-surface-affecting change. Read the full added 'Verification Depth Policy' section directly, cross-checked it line-by-line against the existing Task Safety Classification and Acceptance Boundary Rules sections, and found no contradiction. All six completion criteria are met with direct evidence. Independent local guardrails (validate-cockpit-state, check-schemas, doctor) all clean. Task selection this cycle was owner-directed (the owner named the task ID and source directly), not a PCC self-promotion, so promotion_basis correctly remains null and the auto-promotion gate does not apply.
